data.body:
G3 (child coverage) — parent 5fd2 × child acc1 (Pass-2 verifier): ONE partial-coverage finding.

4-bucket audit of this child's contribution to the parent's three-pass verdict-model AC, Pass-2 clause ("a SEPARATE Pass-2 verifier (ONE aggregate verification pass over ALL findings — not per-finding — agentic/tool-using for codebase-grounded findings and single-turn otherwise, on a non-frontier model) emits severity attributes {...} + the typed binary sub-question set {yes|no|insufficient}"):

- ONE aggregate pass over ALL findings (not per-finding) → FULLY covered (child AC1)
- non-frontier model → FULLY covered (child AC1)
- severity attributes {prod/debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility} → FULLY covered (child AC2)
- typed binary sub-question set {yes|no|insufficient} + independence rules → FULLY covered (child AC2)
- charitable-plan-reading / entailment precision gate → FULLY covered (child AC3)
- discrimination proving-check (true vs planted-false) → FULLY covered (child AC4, wired into 7284)
- **agentic/tool-using-for-codebase-grounded-findings-AND-single-turn-otherwise (the Pass-2 dual-mode dispatch) → PARTIALLY covered / this AC element is UNCOVERED by any acceptance criterion.**

FINDING — the parent's three-pass AC EXPLICITLY mandates Pass-2 be "agentic/tool-using for codebase-grounded findings and single-turn otherwise." This was a hard-won, deliberate design decision on the epic (finalize-session comment: "agentic for codebase-grounded + single-turn otherwise — not a no-tools one-shot"; reinforced in the non-DSO generalization round where the agentic verify CORRECTED Pass-1 over-fires). None of acc1's four ACs assert this dual-mode dispatch. Worse, the child's Scope ("code-citation findings are rare in plan review (grounding owned by E4/G1G2/A1)") DOWNPLAYS — without explicitly disclaiming — the agentic-when-grounded mode the parent requires, which risks a reading where Pass-2 ships single-turn-only. (The child's completion comments confirm the behavior WAS implemented — "agentic (tool-using) when any finding is code-grounded" — but it is asserted by no AC, so nothing pins it against regression.)

- criteria: [G3]
- location: parent AC "Three-pass verdict model implemented ... a SEPARATE Pass-2 verifier (... agentic/tool-using for codebase-grounded findings and single-turn otherwise ...)"; child acc1 Acceptance Criteria (AC1-AC4) + child Scope ("code-citation findings are rare ... grounding owned by E4/G1G2/A1")
- evidence: parent three-pass AC names the agentic-when-code-grounded dispatch as a required property of Pass-2; epic comment 1782020762824709001 ("agentic for codebase-grounded + single-turn otherwise — not a no-tools one-shot"); ABSENCE of any acc1 AC asserting the dual-mode dispatch; the child Scope frames code-grounded findings as out-of-focus, the inverse emphasis of the parent AC.
- scenarios: a future revision of the Pass-2 prompt/runner drops the agentic mode (or never wires tool access) and ships single-turn-only; no acc1 AC catches it, so a codebase-grounded finding (e.g. a cited-reference veto) is verified without ever reading the code — exactly the FP-source the epic's agentic-grounding decision was made to fix.
- impact: a load-bearing parent-AC element (Pass-2 agentic-when-grounded) is unpinned by any acceptance criterion; the verdict-model coverage is incomplete at the criteria level even though the behavior exists in the current implementation.

Sibling cross-check (absence holds across the WHOLE roster): the Pass-2 verifier's own execution-tier dispatch is acc1's sole concern. 1913 owns Pass-1; 487d (Pass-3) only CONSUMES Pass-2's cited_reference_accurate veto, it does not produce the agentic verification; ca03 (orchestrator) wires the passes but does not carry a Pass-2-agentic-mode AC; 7284 owns the eval cases, not the dispatch contract. No sibling backstops this element.

Suggested fix: add an acc1 AC pinning the dual-mode dispatch, e.g. "[ ] Pass-2 runs AGENTIC (tool-using: rebar + filesystem per the AGENT-tier tool-access contract) when ANY finding is codebase-grounded (cites code / answers the cited_reference_accurate veto), and single-turn otherwise — with a proving check that a code-grounded finding triggers the tool-using path." This closes the parent-AC element and protects the agentic-grounding decision against silent regression. (Alternatively, if the design truly intends single-turn-only for plan review, the parent AC's "agentic/tool-using for codebase-grounded findings" clause should be reconciled — but the epic's own finalize round explicitly chose the dual mode, so pinning it on acc1 is the consistent resolution.)
